fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Nebraska Licensed Medical Nutrition Therapist (LMNT) — Requirements & Fees (2026)
seo.description: Apply for a Nebraska LMNT credential via DHHS. $114 application fee. Master's degree, 900+ hours supervised practice, and CDR exam required.
seo.keywords: Nebraska dietitian license, NE LMNT requirements, Nebraska DHHS Medical Nutrition Therapist, Nebraska LMNT application fee

board.name: Medical Nutrition Therapy Licensure
board.parent_dept: Nebraska Department of Health and Human Services (DHHS)

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Every 2 years (by Sept 1 of odd years) + 30 CE hours

license_types.title: Licensed Medical Nutrition Therapist (LMNT)
license_types.description: In Nebraska, dietitians are licensed as LMNTs. An LMNT license is required specifically to provide medical nutrition therapy (MNT), which involves treating diseases or medical conditions.

steps.title: Complete Education & Supervised Practice
steps.description: Earn a master's degree or higher from an ACEND-accredited program. Complete a minimum of 900 hours of board-approved supervised practice through an accredited dietitian internship.

steps.title: Pass the CDR Examination
steps.description: Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) Registration Examination for Dietitians. Verification of current RD or RDN status satisfies this and the education requirements.

steps.title: Submit the Application to DHHS
steps.description: Complete the application form for Medical Nutrition Therapy Licensure and submit it with the $114 fee, proof of age (minimum 19), and proof of U.S. citizenship or lawful presence.

steps.title: Provide Verification & Fingerprints
steps.description: Send a copy of your active CDR card or have primary source verification sent. Starting January 1, 2026, all DHHS applicants must undergo a fingerprint-based criminal background check.

documents.detail: Verification of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
A copy of a birth certificate, driver's license, passport, or similar documentation.
Required for all new applications starting in 2026.

provisional_text: Nebraska does not specify a provisional LMNT license option. An applicant must meet all qualifications including examination prior to licensure.
reciprocity_text: Licensure by reciprocity is available for an applicant currently licensed in another state. The requirements of that state must be substantially equivalent to Nebraska's. Verification of all held licenses and active CDR registration is required, along with the $114 application fee.

faqs.question: How much does a Nebraska LMNT license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial license application fee is $114.

faqs.question: When does the Nebraska license need to be renewed?
faqs.answer: Nebraska LMNT licenses expire on September 1 of all odd-numbered years. The renewal fee is $114.

faqs.question: Is the title 'Dietitian' licensed in Nebraska?
faqs.answer: No, Nebraska licenses the specific practice of Medical Nutrition Therapy, granting the title Licensed Medical Nutrition Therapist (LMNT). General nutrition services may not require a license, but only an LMNT can provide medical nutrition therapy.
